items.correct: Ideological incompatibility plus mutual suspicion after WWII — disputes over Eastern Europe, the atomic bomb, and broken wartime agreements.
items.misconception: Stalin started it by invading Eastern Europe after the war.
items.random_wrong: It began when the USSR shot down a US spy plane.
items.why: Students treat one side as solely responsible. Most historians now see the Cold War as a mutual breakdown — both sides acted on rational fears that the other read as aggression.

items.q: What did the Truman Doctrine commit the US to in 1947?
items.correct: Supporting free peoples resisting communist takeover — initially Greece and Turkey — formalising the policy of containment articulated by George Kennan.
items.misconception: The Truman Doctrine declared war on the Soviet Union.
items.random_wrong: The Truman Doctrine ordered the dropping of the atomic bomb on Japan.
items.why: Containment was political and economic, not military invasion of the USSR. Students conflate Cold War rhetoric with hot war; Truman's doctrine was a framework for indirect rivalry.

items.q: Why was the Berlin Wall built in 1961?
items.correct: To stop the mass flow of East Germans to the West via Berlin (millions had fled), which was draining the GDR of skilled workers and threatening the regime's survival.
items.misconception: The Berlin Wall was built to stop a Western invasion of East Berlin.
items.random_wrong: The Berlin Wall was built by NATO to keep East Germans out of West Germany.
items.why: The Wall faced inward to trap GDR citizens, not outward against invasion. Students miss that it was an admission of weakness — people were voting with their feet.

items.q: Who replaced Roosevelt as US President at the Potsdam Conference?
items.why: It's Truman. “Eisenhower” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what month and year was the Yalta Conference held?
items.why: Correct answer: February 1945. “July 1945” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Into how many zones was Germany to be divided after WWII?
items.why: The real figure is 4. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
